description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with stages prepare, test, publish, and release, using the golang:1.11 Docker image for Go jobs and docker:latest with a docker:dind service for container operations, caching the vendor/ directory with pull policy and running on runners tagged docker. In the test stage define a build job that runs `CGO_ENABLED=0 go build -o build_out/keyforce`, caches vendor/, and publishes the build_out/ directory as an artifact named $CI_COMMIT_SHA that expires in 4 weeks. Also in the test stage add a unittest job that runs `go test ./...` with the same Go image and cache. In the publish stage add a container job that depends on build, runs `docker info`, logs in to $REGISTRY_SERVER using $REGISTRY_USERNAME and $REGISTRY_PASSWORD, writes $CI_BUILD_REF to a VERSION file, builds a Docker image tagged $REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG, pushes it, and logs out. Finally, in the release stage add a release job that runs only on the master branch, depends on container, runs `docker info`, logs in, pulls the previously built image, retags it as $REGISTRY_IMAGE:latest, pushes the latest tag, and logs out.
